# POMS latest analysis
## analysing the poms 1-6 batches dataset
```{python}
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
import scvi
import os
import celltypist
from celltypist import models
import re
import pertpy as pt
import muon as mu
import mudata as md
import sys
```

## load in just the RNA element of the data (will come back to the surface Ab data)
### checking size of object to get a rough idea of what I'm dealing with
```{python}
adata = mu.read(filename = "mudata_poms1-6_adtSoupX.h5mu/rna")
adata
(sys.getsizeof(adata) / (1024**3))
```

## appears Tim has already done some preliminary analysis, so checking the QC metrics to confirm data is filtered
## i also check counts, logcounts and X to confirm normalisation and where the raw data is
```{python}
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(12,8))

# Define a GridSpec with 6 equal-width units
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(2, 6, height_ratios=[1,1.2], figure=fig)

# Top row: 3 violins, each spans 2 units (so all equal width)
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 0:2])
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 2:4])
ax3 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 4:6])

sc.pl.violin(adata, "pct_counts_mt", ax=ax1, show=False, stripplot=False)
sc.pl.violin(adata, "pct_counts_ribo", ax=ax2, show=False, stripplot=False)
sc.pl.violin(adata, "pct_counts_hb", ax=ax3, show=False, stripplot=False)

# Bottom row: 2 histograms, each spans 3 units (1.5 of the top-row plots)
ax4 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 0:3])
ax5 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 3:6])

sns.histplot(np.sum(adata.layers["counts"], axis=1), bins=100, color="blue", ax=ax4)
ax4.set_title("Counts")

sns.histplot(np.sum(adata.layers["logcounts"], axis=1), bins=100, color="red", ax=ax5)
ax5.set_title("Logcounts")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

plt.savefig("POMS_QC_postfiltering.png", dpi=300)
```

## at this point i also extract all of the obs into a df so i can inspect it and check what the data has
### for example im checking how many cells belong to each patient and in which pool they belong
```{python}
obs_df = adata.obs.copy()
counts = obs_df.groupby(['poolID', 'patient_nice']).size().reset_index(name='n_cells')
counts_actual = counts[counts['n_cells'] > 0]
plt.figure()
sns.barplot(data=counts_actual, x='poolID', y='n_cells', hue='patient_nice')
```

## this showed me that there are some duplicate pools, or a situation where a patient was sequencing across two pools, therefore i will rename these to make them usable for batch correction by moving these into one combined pool
```{python}
adata.obs["pool_new"] = adata.obs["poolID"].map(
    {
    "sc12":"sc12_13",
    "sc13":"sc12_13",
    "sc14":"sc14_15",
    "sc15":"sc14_15",
    "sc16":"sc16_17",
    "sc17":"sc16_17",
    "sc18":"sc18_19",
    "sc19":"sc18_19",
    "Pool_3":"Pool_3",
    "Pool_4":"Pool_4",
    }
)
new_counts = adata.obs.groupby(['pool_new', 'patient_nice']).size().reset_index(name='n_cells')
```

## we want to analyse fetal samples separately, so pulling out and re-running processing
```{python}
pom_fetal = adata[adata.obs["fetmat"].isin(["Fetal"])].copy()
del adata
pom_fetal.obs["fetmat"].value_counts()
```

## make sure X is set to the counts and then normalise, before saving the logcounts again
```{python}
pom_fetal.X = pom_fetal.layers["counts"].copy()
sc.pp.normalize_total(pom_fetal)
sc.pp.log1p(pom_fetal)
pom_fetal.layers["logcounts"] = pom_fetal.X.copy()
```

## before batch correction, let's assess the state of the umap to see how strong the effects are by running the basic pipeline
```{python}
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(pom_fetal)
sc.tl.pca(pom_fetal)
sc.pp.neighbors(pom_fetal)
sc.tl.umap(pom_fetal)
pom_fetal
```

## huge batch effects are seen
```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(20,15))
sc.pl.umap(pom_fetal, color=["pool_new"], wspace=1,show=True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("UMAP_uncorrected.png",dpi=300)
```

## first corrective step is to perform batch-aware hvg selection - scanpy has this built in
```{python}
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(
    pom_fetal, n_top_genes=2000, flavor="cell_ranger", batch_key="pool_new"
)
pom_fetal.var
```
```{python}
plt.figure()
n_batches = pom_fetal.var["highly_variable_nbatches"].value_counts()
ax = n_batches.plot(kind="bar")
n_batches
```

## data is then subetted to the hvgs for scvi to be run on - the embeddings will be added back to the full data later
```{python}
pom_hvg = pom_fetal[:, pom_fetal.var["highly_variable"]].copy()
pom_hvg
```

## set up the scvi model
```{python}
scvi.model.SCVI.setup_anndata(pom_hvg, layer="counts", batch_key="pool_new")
pom_hvg
model_scvi = scvi.model.SCVI(pom_hvg)
model_scvi
model_scvi.view_anndata_setup()
```

## automatically calculate the epoch number (eopchs = number of interations the model performs)
```{python}
max_epochs_scvi = np.min([round((20000 / pom_fetal.n_obs) * 400), 400])
max_epochs_scvi
```

## run the model
```{python}
model_scvi.train()
```
